title: API Gateway Request Flow
description: An interactive vis-network diagram showing how an API gateway sits between clients and backend microservices, absorbing the cross-cutting concerns of authentication, rate limiting, routing, and logging.
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Information Systems
Software Architecture
Microservices
API Gateway
learningObjectives: Identify the four cross-cutting concerns an API gateway typically owns
Articulate which concerns belong in the gateway vs the service
Predict the consequences of skipping the gateway pattern
Trace a request through the gateway and back
prerequisites: Familiarity with HTTP and web services
assumptions: Three backend services (Orders, Inventory, Customer), each with its own database
limitations: Does not depict service mesh or sidecar variants
